import pandas as pd
import logging

# Set up basic configuration for logging
logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO, format='%(asctime)s - %(levelname)s - %(message)s')

def task_func(sheet_name, excel_file_location="test.xlsx", csv_file_location="test.csv"):
    try:
        # Read the Excel file
        df = pd.read_excel(excel_file_location)

        # Ensure the specified sheet name exists in the Excel file
        if sheet_name not in df.columns:
            raise ValueError(f"The specified sheet '{sheet_name}' is not found in the Excel file.")

        # Select the specified sheet and convert it to a DataFrame
        sheet = df[sheet_name]

        # Ensure all values are numerical for accurate sum calculations
        if not sheet.select_dtypes(include='number').isnull().sum().any():
            logging.info("All values in the selected sheet are numerical.")
        else:
            raise ValueError("The specified sheet contains non-numerical data. Only numerical data is supported for accurate sum calculations.")

        # Convert the DataFrame to a CSV file
        sheet.to_csv(csv_file_location, index=False)

        # Calculate the sum of each column in the CSV file (now that it's created)
        csv_df = pd.read_csv(csv_file_location)
        column_sums = {column: csv_df[column].sum() for column in csv_df.columns}

        # Return the tuple containing the calculated sums and the path of the generated CSV file
        return tuple(column_sums), csv_file_location
    except FileNotFoundError as e:
        logging.error(f"The Excel file '{excel_file_location}' does not exist at the specified path.")
        raise e
